metadata.description: Fullstack developer and researcher specializing in decentralized identity, SSI, agentic AI systems, and verifiable credentials. Based in Bordeaux, France.

common.noContent: Nothing here yet — check back soon.

home.bio1: I'm a fullstack developer and researcher based in Bordeaux. I fell into computer science young and never left, drawn by the idea that code isn't just tooling, it's infrastructure for society. That curiosity led me to distributed systems, cryptography, and eventually to the question I keep coming back to: how do we establish trust without a central authority?
home.bio2: The more I look at the web's architecture, the clearer the problem becomes. Every system of trust we've built still relies on someone at the center vouching for everyone else: certificate authorities, social login, identity providers. It works, until it doesn't. I'm interested in what comes after.
home.bio3: Today I build production SSI infrastructure at Verana, DIDComm messaging, verifiable credentials, trust registries. I also created Credat, an open-source library that brings the same trust model to AI agents, giving autonomous systems verifiable identities and scoped delegation. As agents become more capable and autonomous, the question of how services verify them, and who authorized them to act, becomes urgent.
home.news1: Joined Verana as fullstack developer — building SSI infrastructure
home.news2: Enrolled in Harvard CS50 — expanding CS fundamentals
home.news3: Founded Klyx — web development agency in Bordeaux
home.news4: Speaker at GDG Bordeaux — presentation on modern web development
home.bio4: I recently joined the Trust over IP Foundation to deepen the research side of this work. I'm 19, investing everything I have in this space, convinced that decentralized identity is one of the foundational problems of the next era. For humans, and for AI agents.
home.bio5: Before tech, I spent seven years training as a high-level parkour athlete, competing and teaching across France. In 2025, I co-founded PKBA, now the largest parkour club in Nouvelle-Aquitaine. Discipline, pattern recognition, and learning from failure fast, sports and systems thinking have more in common than they appear.
home.news5: Published Credat on npm, open-source trust layer for AI agents using DIDs and VCs
home.news6: Co-founded PKBA, the largest parkour club in southwestern France

research.title: Research
research.intro: My research interests lie at the intersection of distributed systems, applied cryptography, and digital identity. I'm particularly interested in systems that give individuals control over their personal data while maintaining interoperability across organizations and borders.
research.area1desc: Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs) provide a foundation for self-sovereign identity by allowing entities to create and control their own identifiers without permission from any central authority. I study the design trade-offs between different DID methods (did:web, did:peer, did:key) and their implications for security, privacy, and scalability.
research.area2desc: Verifiable Credentials enable portable, tamper-evident digital attestations. I'm interested in selective disclosure mechanisms (BBS+ signatures, SD-JWT) that allow holders to share only the minimum necessary information, and in zero-knowledge proof techniques that enable predicate proofs without revealing underlying data.
research.area3desc: Decentralized systems still need trust anchors. I work on trust registry architectures that allow ecosystems to define and enforce governance rules — who can issue what, which credentials are accepted where, and how trust decisions are made at scale.
research.area4desc: DIDComm is a messaging protocol that enables secure, private communication between identity agents. I work on production implementations of DIDComm v2, including message routing, encryption, and protocol negotiation for credential exchange workflows.
research.question1: How can trust registries be made both decentralized and practically governable?
research.question2: What are the formal privacy guarantees of different selective disclosure schemes under realistic adversarial models?
research.question3: How can SSI systems achieve meaningful interoperability across jurisdictions with different legal frameworks?
research.question4: What are effective revocation mechanisms that preserve holder privacy?

cv.title: Curriculum Vitae
cv.exp1desc: Built the Verana Visualizer, a real-time blockchain dashboard with 3D network visualization deployed across multi-cloud infrastructure. Developed the W3C-compliant Verifiable Credentials frontend and wallet integration. Architected Concieragent, an AI-powered assistant orchestrating 6 MCP servers with DIDComm messaging and multi-LLM routing.
cv.exp2desc: Founded a web development agency serving 5+ clients with 100% satisfaction. Delivered production sites scoring >95 on Lighthouse, achieving +80% organic traffic growth and +35% lead generation for clients.
cv.exp3desc: Developed Hoppy, a voice-powered real estate assistant using ElevenLabs and LLM integration across 28K+ property listings. Built AI marketing agents and redesigned the homepage, tripling website traffic.
cv.edu1desc: RNCP Level 7 Title & BTS SIO. 5-year software engineering program. Specialization in web development and distributed systems.
cv.edu2desc: Fundamental CS concepts: algorithms, data structures, systems programming.
cv.edu3desc: General Baccalauréat with honors, specializing in Mathematics and Physics.
cv.skill1: Languages & Frameworks: TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Next.js, React, Node.js, HTML/CSS, LaTeX
cv.skill2: Infrastructure & DevOps: Docker, Kubernetes, Helm, CI/CD (GitHub Actions), Git, MySQL/SQL
cv.skill3: Web3 & Architecture: COSMOS SDK, Verifiable Credentials, DIDs, MCP (Model Context Protocol), DIDComm
cv.skill4: Soft Skills: Self-discipline, Rigor, Project Management, Problem Solving, Entrepreneurial Mindset, Digital Marketing, Teamwork
cv.proj1desc: Contributed to 10+ projects including work with the OpenWallet Foundation. Creator of Credat.io (verifiable credentials platform) and Face10ai (AI-powered tool).
cv.proj2desc: Founded an e-commerce brand centered around the Parkour and Movement community.
cv.proj3desc: Co-founded and served as treasurer of a Parkour club with 65+ active members.
cv.proj4desc: Active member of Google Developer Group Bordeaux, participating in and contributing to tech meetups.
